questions: Q1: Should library commands be a new module or extend the existing comments module?
Q2: How should library item references be resolved in the CLI?

answers.answer: A1: A new src/library/ module with nested subcommands. Library items are a distinct domain — they need list/show/comments subcommands separate from ticket comments. The existing comments module is hard-coupled to --ticket flag and ticket API endpoints. A new module follows the same structural pattern (index.ts router, subcommand files, flag parsing) but routes to /api/library/* endpoints.
evidence: src/index.ts: switch-based routing with separate modules per domain (tickets, comments, inspect, skill)
src/comments/index.ts: router dispatches to list.ts and post.ts using ticketId — not extensible to library without breaking changes
src/tickets/index.ts: ticket commands follow list/get/create pattern — library module follows the same structure

answers.answer: A2: Library items should be resolvable by: (1) Library item ID (cuid), (2) ticket short ID (e.g., 'RSH-439') which maps to the latest library item for that ticket, (3) partial title match as a fallback. A new resolve-library-item.ts utility follows the resolve-ticket.ts pattern: first try exact ID, then try short ID via the /api/library/items?ticketId= endpoint (already exists), then fallback to listing items and matching by title substring.
evidence: src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts: accepts internal ID, short ID, or ticket number — same multi-strategy pattern
Client api/library.ts L38-47: libraryItemByTicketQueryOptions uses /library/items?ticketId= — server already supports ticket-based lookup
Ticket description: agents need programmatic access — stable IDs and human-readable short IDs are both important

statement: The CLI requires a new src/library/ module with list, show, and comments (list/post) subcommands following the existing module pattern. Section targeting uses --section with heading slugs. Rating uses --rating with human-readable values (thumbs-up, love, thumbs-down). Item resolution supports ID, ticket short ID, and title match. SKILL.md must document all new commands for agent discoverability. All commands depend on server API endpoints.
